news.title:
InterGlobe Aviation fell 5.5% to Rs 3,920 as a foreign brokerage cut its target price from Rs 6,000 to Rs 5,200 due to rising fuel costs and Middle East tensions.
Brokerage projects FY26 EBITDAR of Rs 13,700 cr, highlights IndiGo's market position, fuel surcharge, and robust cash balance despite 77.6% Q3 profit drop.
news.summary:
InterGlobe Aviation fell 5.5% to Rs 3,920 as a foreign brokerage cut its target price from Rs 6,000 to Rs 5,200 due to rising fuel costs and Middle East tensions.
Brokerage projects FY26 EBITDAR of Rs 13,700 cr, highlights IndiGo's market position, fuel surcharge, and robust cash balance despite 77.6% Q3 profit drop.

news.title: IndiGo shares fall 6%, hitting a 52-week low after Goldman Sachs cuts earnings forecast.
news.summary: IndiGo shares fall 6%, hitting a 52-week low after Goldman Sachs cuts earnings forecast.

news.title: Goldman Sachs remains positive on IndiGo but cuts target to ₹5,200.

news.title:
SpiceJet dropped 10%, hitting Rs 10.85, IndiGo fell 6% to Rs 3,895 amid ATF price hike fears.
Iran-US tensions and oil price surge drive concerns, impacting Indian airline stocks.
news.summary:

news.title:
IndiGo, SpiceJet, and Air India oppose govt's 60% free seat selection rule.
Airlines argue the mandate will force airfare hikes for passengers.
news.summary:

news.title:
Govt removes domestic airfare caps, allowing airlines to adjust costs.
IndiGo and others may raise fares as operating costs escalate.
news.summary:
Govt removes domestic airfare caps, allowing airlines to adjust costs.
IndiGo and others may raise fares as operating costs escalate.

news.title:
West Asia conflict raises challenges for airlines, including airspace curbs, high fuel costs, and surging insurance premiums.
IndiGo, with 18% revenue from West Asia, faces fuel cost pressure but can offset via its 64% domestic market share.
news.summary:
West Asia conflict raises challenges for airlines, including airspace curbs, high fuel costs, and surging insurance premiums.

news.title:
ICRA places InterGlobe Aviation's long-term ratings on watch with negative implications, citing geopolitical risks in West Asia.
IndiGo faces pressure from elevated fuel costs, rupee depreciation, and airspace restrictions impacting profitability and operations.

news.title:
DGCA seat rules unlikely to impact IndiGo revenues; fare hikes may offset losses.
IndiGo could gain Rs 4,000 crore in annual revenue, says Asian Mkts Securities.

news.title:
Citi targets IndiGo shares at ₹5,100; flights to West Asia cut to 36 daily by March 2026.

Expensive travel emerges as airlines pass increased costs to customers.

news.title:
IndiGo runs 252 weekly Middle East flights from Mar 16-28, including 126 to/from Saudi, 28 Oman, 98 UAE.
Flights to Doha, Kuwait, Bahrain, Dammam, Fujairah, Ras Al Khaimah, Sharjah suspended; risks monitored.
news.summary:

news.title:
India's market remains expensive despite valuation decline from West Asia crisis; PE ratio higher due to strong corporate balance sheets.

news.title:
IndiGo to add fuel charges from 14 March 2026, ranging ₹425-₹2300/sector.
